pattern: Track only the set of distinct OR values produced by subarrays ending at the current element; that set has at most 30 members because every step can only set bits, never clear them.
hints: OR-ing more elements only turns bits on, so the distinct OR values of subarrays ending at position i form a small set — at most as many bits as the element width.
Build the set of OR values ending at each position by OR-ing each element in the previous set with the current value, then union that into a global result set.
Maintain a running set `cur` of ORs for subarrays ending here: `cur = {x | y for y in cur} | {x}`. Union `cur` into `res` each step; answer is `len(res)`.

edge_inputs.description: single zero element — one distinct OR value
all identical elements — OR never grows beyond the single value
two elements that share no bits so all four subarray ORs are distinct
strictly increasing powers of two produce the maximum number of distinct ORs
repeated pair with overlapping bits where subarrays share OR values
array of zeros — only one distinct OR value regardless of length
large value alongside small values to test bit-width handling

stress.description: random array over the full value range to stress the bit-tracking set
stress.note: large n with wide values exercises the O(30n) DP

stress.description: array of powers of two to maximise the number of distinct OR values at each position
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    bits = list(range(30))
    rng.shuffle(bits)
    return [[1 << b for b in bits[:size]]]
stress.note: 30 distinct power-of-two bits produce many distinct OR combinations
